draft.introduction: Mark’s resurrection account is famously abrupt. The women find the tomb empty, a young man announces Jesus has risen, and they flee in fear. The oldest manuscripts end at 16:8.

translation.text: When the Sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices, so that they might go and anoint him. And entering the tomb, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, dressed in a white robe, and they were alarmed. And he said to them, “Do not be alarmed. You seek Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has risen; he is not here.” And they went out and fled from the tomb, for trembling and astonishment had seized them, and they said nothing to anyone, for they were afraid.

translator_notes.note: Codex Sinaiticus ends Mark’s Gospel at 16:8 with ἐφοβοῦντο γάρ (“for they were afraid”). The longer ending (16:9-20) found in later manuscripts is absent. Most scholars consider 16:8 the original ending—a deliberately open, unsettling conclusion.
